case_name: Texas Children's Hospital v. Tarshell Scott, Individually and as Surviving Parent and Representative as a Surviving Parent and Representative of the Estate of D. S., a Minor

summary.brief: Texas Children's Hospital v. Tarshell Scott, Individually and as Surviving Parent and Representative as a Surviving Parent and Representative of the Estate of D. S., a Minor. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: Tarshell Scott’s disabled 17-year-old daughter was discharged from Texas Children’s Hospital (“TCH”) with a laceration to her labia that required readmittance within 24 hours.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: D.S. later died for reasons unrelated to the subject matter of the lawsuit.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: D.S. was admitted to TCH from October 11, 2021, to October 20, 2021, for increased respiratory support.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The hospital discharged D.S. around 11:00 a.m. on October 20, 2021, and Scott noticed blood spotting in D.S.’s diaper when they arrived home.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Scott took D.S. back to TCH. D.S. had a fever when she arrived at the emergency department, and the hospital performed an infectious workup.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Carroll’s report was a good faith effort with regard to the claims of negligence and gross negligence.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We affirm the trial court’s order.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: We conclude that the expert report informed TCH of the specific conduct Scott has called into question and provided a basis for the trial court to conclude that the claims have merit.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: D.S., a 17-year-old with a history of static cerebral palsy due to neonatal HSV encephalitis and functional quadriplegia, was admitted for increased respiratory support. She was diagnosed with rhinovirus, H. Influenzae pneumonia, and a urinary tract infection.
past_medical_history: Acute and chronic hypoxemic and hypercarbic respiratory failure

focused_exam.genitourinary: No rash in the pubic area; documented as normal aside from incontinence and usage of diapers

requestables.name: Pediatric Gynecology Examination
requestables.description: Examination of the vaginal area for laceration

reveal.result_summary: Healing granulation tissue indicating the injury was more than 24 hours old
reveal.clinical_significance: Indicates the injury was not self-inflicted and occurred during hospitalization
reveal.decision_impact: Prompted involvement of Child Protection Team

timeline_phases.name: Initial Hospitalization
timeline_phases.description: Patient admitted for increased respiratory support

timeline_phases.name: Discharge and Readmission
timeline_phases.description: Patient discharged and readmitted with laceration

decision_points.prompt: What should have been done to prevent the oversight of the laceration?

options.description: Conduct thorough genitourinary examination and document findings
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard care requires thorough examination and documentation to prevent oversight of injuries

options.description: Rely on routine diaper changes to identify any issues

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant relied on routine diaper changes

expected_action_court.description: Thorough examination and documentation should have been conducted
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established the need for thorough examination

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to conduct thorough examination led to oversight of significant injury
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient readmitted with significant laceration requiring treatment

key_findings: Trial court's denial of motion to dismiss affirmed

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Proper documentation could have prevented readmission

factual_timeline.event: D.S. admitted to TCH for increased respiratory support
D.S. readmitted to TCH with fever and laceration

court_findings.legal_significance: Trial court's denial of motion to dismiss affirmed
